failure_surface: Agent created formulas that incorrectly mapped categories - SUMPRODUCT formulas reference wrong source columns for question-to-category lookup, resulting in zero matches even though valid question-answer pairs exist in the spreadsheet.
root_cause: Agent failed to correctly identify where question-to-category mappings are stored in the worksheet. The exploration code assumed category assignments were distributed throughout columns E-H across rows 5-84, but the actual category definition structure differs from this assumption, leading to empty category lists and ultimately incorrect formulas that reference mismatched cell ranges.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before constructing summary formulas, the agent must programmatically verify the complete location and format of metadata/lookup tables (question-to-category mappings) by scanning multiple candidate areas (header rows, dedicated lookup sections, hidden rows) rather than assuming a single column-wise distribution pattern.
evidence: Step 2 exploration output shows all four category lists empty: Activist [], Reflector [], Theorist [], Pragmatist []
Step 2 logic checks columns E-H for question number matches: if e_val == q_num_int, but e_val was None for all examined rows
Row 4 in the raw data shows category-related values in columns F-H-I (values 3, 7, 1, 5) that may represent question groupings, but agent did not incorporate this finding into formula design
Final formulas reference F5:F84, G5:G84 etc., but these ranges do not contain the actual question-to-category relationship data needed for MATCH() to work correctly

memory_items.title: Metadata Location Verification
memory_items.lesson: Always scan for metadata/lookup tables beyond obvious data rows - they may exist in header regions, merged cells, or non-contiguous ranges
memory_items.generalization: When a spreadsheet contains derived calculations based on classification rules, first map WHERE those rules are stored by examining header rows, named ranges, and any rows/columns with non-standard formatting or labels indicating lookup/grouping information.

memory_items.title: Category Mapping Validation Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Never assume category-to-item relationships follow a simple column-per-category layout without empirical verification
memory_items.generalization: For surveys or assessments with multiple scoring categories, explicitly test all possible storage patterns: item ID → category columns (many-one), category header row with IDs below (one-many), or external lookup tables. Use print statements to show actual values found, not just None/null results.

memory_items.title: Formula Range Sanity Check
memory_items.lesson: Before writing formulas, verify that referenced ranges contain the expected non-null values
memory_items.generalization: After exploring a worksheet, always confirm that any range used in a formula (like SUMPRODUCT MATCH criteria) actually contains data. If exploration reveals None/empty values in supposed data columns, search adjacent cells, prior rows, or alternative sheet structures before proceeding.

edits.content:
## 6. Metadata & Lookup Table Discovery
- **Classification Scans**: When building aggregation formulas, first identify WHERE classification/mapping data lives. Check header rows (rows 1-4), merged cells, named ranges, and rows with summary labels before data begins.
- **Non-Contiguous Patterns**: Category-to-item mappings may not follow simple column-per-category layouts. Test: item-ID column + category columns (many-one), category-row header with items below (one-many), or separate lookup table sheets.
- **Empty Range Alert**: If exploration returns empty lists or all-None values from expected lookup columns, search adjacent cells, previous rows, and examine the row immediately preceding data section. Adjust formula ranges accordingly before construction.
